findings.title: resolve_instruments() silently continues with empty instrument list after exhausting retries
findings.description: After all retries are exhausted (line 183-185), resolve_instruments() logs a warning and proceeds with an empty instrument list. The gateway then starts CTP and subscribes to zero instruments, making it effectively useless but still running. This violates fail-fast principles and wastes resources.
findings.fix_suggestion: Throw an exception after max_retries if instruments_.empty() and cfg_.underlyings is non-empty, or add a config flag `require_instruments` to control whether empty resolution is fatal.

findings.title: MdApi init failure throws but TdApi state may leak if MdApi throws after TdApi was initialized
findings.description: TdApi is initialized first (lines 217-245) and its resources are cleaned on catch. However, if TdApi init succeeds and then MdApi init (line 248) throws, the catch block at line 262 only cleans MdApi resources. The TdApi resources (td_api_, td_spi_) remain allocated. Since the exception propagates out of init_ctp(), the destructor may or may not clean them depending on how far start() progressed.
findings.fix_suggestion: Restructure init_ctp() with a RAII guard or scope_exit pattern that cleans up ALL CTP resources (both Td and Md) on any failure path. Alternatively, have cleanup_ctp() handle partial states correctly.

findings.title: cleanup_ctp() is not exception-safe — destructor calls it without catch
findings.description: cleanup_ctp() is called from the destructor (line 31) and from stop(). If md_api_->Release() or td_api_->Release() throws (possible with buggy DLLs), the destructor would call std::terminate(). The function is not marked noexcept and has no internal try-catch.
findings.fix_suggestion: Mark cleanup_ctp() as noexcept and wrap the entire body in a try-catch-all block that swallows exceptions. Log errors within the catch block using async-signal-safe operations if called from destructor context.

findings.title: Empty catch-all in on_quote_received suppresses all payload parsing errors silently
findings.description: The catch (...) at line 283 silently swallows std::bad_any_cast and any other exceptions during instrument_id extraction. If the payload structure is corrupted or keys are missing, the tick is silently misrouted as futures (since instrument_id remains empty). This makes production debugging extremely difficult.
findings.fix_suggestion: Log the exception type and message in the catch block, or increment a parsing_error_count_ metric. Alternatively, separate the 'key not found' case from the 'wrong type' case and handle them differently.

findings.title: option_dispatch_loop catches only std::exception, unknown exceptions call std::terminate
findings.description: Line 338 catches `const std::exception&` but not all exceptions. If request_event() throws a non-standard exception (e.g., from a buggy callback), std::terminate() is called, killing the entire process without cleanup.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add a catch-all handler after the std::exception catch: `catch (...) { /* log and continue */ }` to prevent process termination.

findings.title: stop() may deadlock if called from within option_dispatch_thread_
findings.description: stop() calls join() on option_dispatch_thread_ (line 366). If stop() is invoked from within the option_dispatch_thread_ itself (e.g., via a signal handler that runs on that thread), join() on the current thread is undefined behavior. The signal handler in main.cpp calls g_gateway->stop() which could execute on any thread.
findings.fix_suggestion: Before joining, check `if (std::this_thread::get_id() != option_dispatch_thread_.get_id())`. If called from the same thread, use detach() instead of join() and rely on the exit flag.

findings.title: option_dispatch_loop uses synchronous request_event() which blocks the dispatch thread
findings.description: Line 337 calls request_event() synchronously with a 10-second timeout. If the greeks_engine is slow or dead, the dispatch thread is blocked for up to 10 seconds, causing the option_queue_ to back up. This is by design but the queue can still overflow.
findings.fix_suggestion: Consider using an async pattern or a thread pool for compute_greeks requests so that slow responses don't stall the dispatch of subsequent ticks. Document this design trade-off.

findings.title: ctp_gateway.h includes md_spi.h and td_spi.h which pull in CTP headers
findings.description: Lines 3-4 include md_spi.h and td_spi.h, which in turn include ThostFtdcMdApi.h and ThostFtdcTraderApi.h. This means every translation unit that includes ctp_gateway.h transitively includes the entire CTP API, increasing compile times and coupling.
findings.fix_suggestion: Forward-declare MdSpiImpl and TdSpiImpl in ctp_gateway.h and move the md_spi.h/td_spi.h includes to ctp_gateway.cpp. Use std::unique_ptr with the default deleter (which supports incomplete types in the destructor).

findings.title: Raw pointers for CTP API objects instead of RAII wrappers
findings.description: md_api_ and td_api_ are raw pointers (CThostFtdcMdApi*, CThostFtdcTraderApi*). While cleanup_ctp() manually calls Release(), if an exception is thrown between creation and cleanup, or if the destructor path is bypassed, these leak. The SPI objects (md_spi_, td_spi_) use unique_ptr but the API objects don't.
findings.fix_suggestion: Create custom deleters and use std::unique_ptr: `struct CtpMdApiDeleter { void operator()(CThostFtdcMdApi* p) const { if (p) p->Release(); } };` and `std::unique_ptr<CThostFtdcMdApi, CtpMdApiDeleter> md_api_;`. Same for td_api_.

findings.title: safe_copy helper is duplicated in md_spi.cpp and td_spi.cpp
findings.description: The safe_copy function (lines 6-10) is defined identically in both md_spi.cpp and td_spi.cpp. This violates DRY and creates maintenance risk if the helper needs to change.
findings.fix_suggestion: Extract safe_copy (and safe_string) to a shared header (e.g., ctp_utils.h) or an anonymous namespace in a common .cpp file.

findings.title: safe_string helper defined after its first use
findings.description: safe_string is defined at line 133 but first used at line 70 in OnRspUserLogin. In C++, functions must be declared before use. While the compiler may accept this in a single translation unit if it's all in one file, it's poor practice and would fail if the function were in a header.
findings.fix_suggestion: Move safe_string definition before OnRspUserLogin, or add a forward declaration at the top of the file.

findings.title: depth_to_payload does not validate or filter NaN/invalid double values
findings.description: CTP sends NaN or sentinel values (e.g., DBL_MAX) for missing fields. These are blindly inserted into the Payload as doubles (lines 143, 145, 149, etc.). Downstream consumers may not handle NaN correctly, causing silent calculation errors.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add NaN/invalid value filtering: `if (!std::isnan(d->LastPrice)) p["last_price"] = d->LastPrice;` or use std::optional in the Payload value type to represent missing values explicitly.

findings.title: MdSpiImpl should be marked final
findings.description: MdSpiImpl inherits from CThostFtdcMdSpi but is not intended for further inheritance. Marking it final enables compiler devirtualization optimizations and clarifies intent.
findings.fix_suggestion: Change to `class MdSpiImpl final : public CThostFtdcMdSpi`. Same for TdSpiImpl.

findings.title: Atomic members use default memory_order_seq_cst unnecessarily
findings.description: logged_in_.load() and req_id_ increments use the default seq_cst ordering. For simple flags and counters, memory_order_relaxed is sufficient and avoids unnecessary memory barriers on every call.
findings.fix_suggestion: Use `logged_in_.load(std::memory_order_relaxed)` and `req_id_.fetch_add(1, std::memory_order_relaxed)` where sequential consistency is not required.

findings.title: Inconsistent callback member naming: on_quote_cb_
findings.description: The member `on_quote_cb_` uses a different naming convention than `cfg_` or `md_api_`. The `_cb_` suffix is Hungarian-notation-like and less readable than `quote_handler_`.
findings.fix_suggestion: Rename to `quote_handler_` for consistency with modern C++ naming conventions.

findings.title: safe_copy duplicated in td_spi.cpp (same as md_spi.cpp)
findings.description: The same safe_copy function is defined in both td_spi.cpp and md_spi.cpp. This is code duplication that should be centralized.
findings.fix_suggestion: Extract to a shared utility header as recommended for md_spi.cpp.

findings.title: do_login in td_spi.cpp is nearly identical to md_spi.cpp
findings.description: The do_login function populates CThostFtdcReqUserLoginField and calls ReqUserLogin. The logic is duplicated between MdSpiImpl and TdSpiImpl with only the API pointer type differing.
findings.fix_suggestion: Extract a template function or shared helper for CTP login field population.

findings.title: TdSpiImpl lacks OnRspError override
findings.description: CThostFtdcTraderSpi has an OnRspError callback for errors not associated with a specific request. Not overriding it means these errors are silently dropped, creating an operational blind spot.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add `void OnRspError(CThostFtdcRspInfoField* pRspInfo, int nRequestID, bool bIsLast) override;` and log the error details. Same for MdSpiImpl.

findings.title: Library handles leaked on exception after successful open_lib
findings.description: open_lib() returns a raw void* handle. If get_sym() throws (lines 238, 255) or if CreateFtdcMdApi returns null (line 242), the handle is leaked because there is no RAII wrapper to call FreeLibrary/dlclose.
findings.fix_suggestion: Create a RAII wrapper class (e.g., `class DynLib { void* handle_; public: ~DynLib() { if (handle_) close(handle_); } };`) and use it in create_md_api/create_td_api.

findings.title: Forward declarations use struct instead of class
findings.description: Lines 7-8 forward-declare CThostFtdcMdApi and CThostFtdcTraderApi as structs. The CTP headers define them as classes. While C++ allows this mismatch, it's technically incorrect and could confuse readers.
findings.fix_suggestion: Change to `class CThostFtdcMdApi; class CThostFtdcTraderApi;` for correctness.

findings.title: Config validation is minimal — no validation of required fields or ranges
findings.description: GatewayConfig::from_file() parses JSON but does not validate that required fields (md_front, broker_id, user_id) are non-empty. It also doesn't validate that engine_port is in [1, 65535] or that reconnect_interval_secs is positive.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add a validate() method to GatewayConfig that checks all required fields and numeric ranges. Call it at the end of from_file() before returning.

findings.title: GatewayConfig lacks explicit noexcept move operations
findings.description: The struct relies on compiler-generated special members. While std::map move is noexcept in C++17, explicit `GatewayConfig(GatewayConfig&&) noexcept = default;` would document and guarantee this.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add explicit noexcept move constructor and move assignment for clarity and to ensure move semantics are preserved if members change.

findings.title: Global atomic pointer g_gateway still has lifetime race condition
findings.description: g_gateway stores a pointer to a stack-allocated CtpGateway (line 99). While it's now std::atomic, the signal handler (lines 35-39) can still dereference it after the gateway has been destroyed but before g_gateway is set to nullptr (line 105). The window between gateway destruction and g_gateway.store(nullptr) is a race.
findings.fix_suggestion: Use a scope guard or RAII wrapper that guarantees g_gateway is nullified before gateway goes out of scope, even if an exception is thrown. Example: `struct GatewayGuard { CtpGateway* gw; ~GatewayGuard() { g_gateway.store(nullptr); } };`

findings.title: signal() used instead of sigaction() on POSIX
findings.description: Line 84-85 use the C-style signal() function which has implementation-defined behavior on POSIX (handler may be reset to SIG_DFL after first signal). sigaction() provides reliable, portable signal handling.
findings.fix_suggestion: On non-Windows platforms, use sigaction(): `struct sigaction sa; sa.sa_handler = signal_handler; sigemptyset(&sa.sa_mask); sa.sa_flags = 0; sigaction(SIGINT, &sa, nullptr); sigaction(SIGTERM, &sa, nullptr);`

findings.title: No scope guard for g_gateway nullification on exception
findings.description: If gateway.run() throws, the catch block at line 108 handles the exception but g_gateway is never set back to nullptr. While the process exits anyway, this pattern is unsafe if main() were ever restructured to continue.
findings.fix_suggestion: Use the RAII GatewayGuard pattern recommended above to ensure g_gateway is always nullified, even on exceptions.

findings.title: No health check or watchdog after gateway.run() starts
findings.description: After gateway.run() starts, the main thread blocks indefinitely. If internal threads die (ZMQ disconnect, CTP disconnect), there is no mechanism to detect this and exit gracefully.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add a watchdog thread that periodically checks gateway.is_running() and exits with an error code if the gateway stops unexpectedly. Alternatively, have run() return a status code.

findings.title: resolve_instruments() is a very long function with deep nesting
findings.description: The function is ~124 lines with 4+ levels of nested loops and conditionals. It handles retry logic, futures query, options query per exchange with exchange-specific rules, error handling, and logging. This violates SRP and makes testing difficult.
findings.fix_suggestion: Extract helpers: `query_futures_for_product()`, `query_options_for_product()`, `derive_option_products(exchange, product)`, `should_retry_error(e)`. Aim for each function < 50 lines.

findings.title: Magic numbers for retry logic not named constants
findings.description: Lines 71-72 use `const int max_retries = 12` and `const int retry_interval_secs = 5`. These should be `constexpr` and ideally configurable.
findings.fix_suggestion: Change to `static constexpr int max_retries = 12;` and add to GatewayConfig if tunability is desired.

findings.title: Exception string matching for 'timed out' is fragile
findings.description: Line 114 checks `what.find("timed out")` to detect timeout exceptions. This relies on the exact exception message text from request_event(), which could change. If the message changes, retry logic breaks silently.
findings.fix_suggestion: Use a custom exception type (e.g., `tyche::TimeoutError`) or check for a specific error code. Avoid string matching on exception messages.

findings.title: Exchange-specific option product derivation is hardcoded
findings.description: Lines 128-137 hardcode exchange-specific rules for option product IDs (SHFE/DCE use _o, CZCE uses C/P, CFFEX uses same). These business rules may change and should be configurable or centralized.
findings.fix_suggestion: Move the exchange->option_product mapping to a static lookup table or configuration file. Document each exchange's rules clearly.

findings.title: extract_instrument_ids throws on malformed response instead of returning empty
findings.description: The function uses `response.at("result")` and `std::any_cast` without try-catch. If the response format is wrong, it throws std::out_of_range or std::bad_any_cast. While the caller (resolve_instruments) catches std::exception, this mixes control flow with exceptions.
findings.fix_suggestion: Consider returning `std::optional<std::vector<std::string>>` and using `find()` instead of `at()` to avoid exceptions for expected missing keys.

findings.title: on_quote_received copies payload into queue instead of moving
findings.description: Line 297 pushes payload by value: `option_queue_.push(payload)`. Since payload is const&, this copies. For high-frequency market data, this adds overhead.
findings.fix_suggestion: If the callback signature can be changed, accept by value and move: `void on_quote_received(tyche::Payload payload)` then `option_queue_.push(std::move(payload));`. Alternatively, document that copies are intentional for thread safety.

findings.title: Magic numbers in option_dispatch_loop not named constants
findings.description: Line 325 uses `std::chrono::milliseconds(200)`, line 337 uses `10.0f`, and line 296 uses `10000`. These are magic numbers without named constants.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add static constexpr members: `static constexpr auto DISPATCH_WAIT_MS = std::chrono::milliseconds(200); static constexpr float COMPUTE_GREEKS_TIMEOUT = 10.0f; static constexpr size_t MAX_OPTION_QUEUE_SIZE = 10000;`

findings.title: Queue overflow drops newest ticks instead of oldest
findings.description: When the option queue is full (>=10000), new ticks are silently dropped (line 298-300). For market data, older ticks are less valuable than newer ones. Dropping the newest means stale data is processed while fresh data is lost.
findings.fix_suggestion: Use a circular buffer with overwrite semantics, or pop the oldest before pushing the newest when full.

findings.title: start() is not thread-safe — no mutex protecting lifecycle
findings.description: start() uses `started_.exchange(true)` as a guard, but there is no mutex protecting the entire initialization sequence. If two threads call start() concurrently, or if stop() is called mid-start, races on instruments_, option_instruments_, and the CTP API objects can occur.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add a std::mutex start_stop_mtx_ and lock it in both start() and stop(). Document that start()/stop() must be called sequentially.

findings.title: Destructor logic is complex and duplicates stop()/cleanup_ctp()
findings.description: The destructor checks if the thread is joinable and either calls stop() or cleanup_ctp(). This logic is fragile: if the thread is joinable but stop() throws, the destructor calls std::terminate(). If the thread is not joinable, it only cleans CTP resources without stopping the base class.
findings.fix_suggestion: Simplify: always call stop() in the destructor, and ensure stop() is noexcept. Or better, use the RAII pattern for all resources and let the member destructors handle cleanup in the correct order.

findings.title: TdApi login timeout is logged but not treated as fatal
findings.description: If td_spi_->wait_for_login() returns false (line 229-233), an error is logged but initialization continues. The TdApi object remains allocated but in a non-logged-in state. Subsequent operations may fail obscurely.
findings.fix_suggestion: Treat TdApi login timeout as fatal if cfg_.td_front is explicitly configured, or expose the connection status via a health check. Document the behavior when TdApi is optional.

findings.title: TdApi partial initialization on catch does not reset td_spi_ safely
findings.description: In the TdApi catch block (lines 234-244), td_api_ is released and nulled, but td_spi_ is only reset. If td_spi_ holds a reference to td_api_ (it does via td_api_ member), resetting the unique_ptr destroys the SPI, which may try to access the already-released td_api_.
findings.fix_suggestion: Unregister the SPI from the API before releasing the API: `td_api_->RegisterSpi(nullptr);` is already done, but ensure TdSpiImpl destructor doesn't touch td_api_. Document this invariant or use the RAII wrapper pattern.

findings.title: Mixed Chinese and English comments without documented convention
findings.description: The codebase uses Chinese comments extensively (e.g., '// 步骤 2：向 static_data 查询期货 + 期权合约列表'). Some files (ctp_loader.h) use English exclusively. There is no documented convention for when to use which language.
findings.fix_suggestion: Establish a comment language convention in the project style guide. Consider translating public API comments to English for international contributors.

findings.title: Comment at line 71 says '12 * 5s = 60s' but loop runs 13 times
findings.description: The loop is `for (int attempt = 0; attempt <= max_retries; ++attempt)` where max_retries = 12. This means 13 iterations total (0 through 12), giving 65 seconds of waiting, not 60.
findings.fix_suggestion: Fix the comment to '13 * 5s = 65s' or change the loop to `< max_retries` for 12 attempts.

findings.title: std::cout used for logging instead of structured logger
findings.description: The codebase uses std::cout/std::cerr for all logging. This lacks log levels, timestamps, rotation, and structured output (JSON) that production systems need.
findings.fix_suggestion: Integrate a lightweight logging library (spdlog) or the project's existing logging framework. At minimum, prefix logs with timestamps and severity levels.

findings.title: No unit tests for resolve_instruments retry logic
findings.description: The retry loop with fixed 5s intervals is untested. Testing would require mocking request_event() to fail N times then succeed.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add unit tests using a mock TycheModule: (1) immediate success, (2) success after N retries, (3) total failure, (4) mixed success/failure across exchanges.

findings.title: on_quote_received routing logic is untested
findings.description: The futures vs options routing decision based on option_instruments_ membership is critical and untested. A bug would send all ticks to the wrong destination.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add unit tests that inject mock payloads with known instrument IDs and verify routing to send_event() vs option queue.

findings.title: depth_to_payload conversion is untested
findings.description: The CTP struct-to-Payload conversion is critical data transformation code with no automated tests. A field mapping error (swapping bid/ask) would only be caught in production.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add unit tests that construct CThostFtdcDepthMarketDataField with known values, call depth_to_payload, and verify each key maps correctly with correct types.

findings.title: find_export_containing PE parsing is untested
findings.description: The Windows-specific PE export table parsing has no unit tests. Testing malformed DLL scenarios requires mock PE files.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add integration tests with mock DLLs/SOs that test: (1) normal export resolution, (2) mangled name fallback, (3) malformed PE handling, (4) bounds check validation.

findings.title: option_instruments_ is read with shared_lock but never written after start
findings.description: option_instruments_ is populated during resolve_instruments() and never modified after. The shared_mutex provides thread-safe reads, but since there are no writers after start(), a simple const reference or atomic pointer would suffice.
findings.fix_suggestion: Document the immutable-after-start invariant. If truly immutable, consider populating it before the dispatch thread starts and accessing without locks.

findings.title: option_dispatch_loop thread name not set
findings.description: The dispatch thread is not given a name. For debugging and profiling (e.g., in perf or Visual Studio), named threads are essential.
findings.fix_suggestion: Set the thread name at the start of option_dispatch_loop(): on Linux use `pthread_setname_np(pthread_self(), "ctp_opt_dispatch");`, on Windows use `SetThreadDescription(GetCurrentThread(), L"ctp_opt_dispatch");`.

findings.title: option_err_count_ suppression threshold is hardcoded to 5
findings.description: The error suppression logic at lines 341-346 uses a hardcoded threshold of 5. This should be configurable.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add a config field `option_err_suppress_threshold` or a named constant.

findings.title: cleanup_ctp() resets md_spi_ after md_api_ but order is not documented
findings.description: Lines 389-390 reset SPI objects after API objects are released (lines 379-388). This ordering assumes SPI destructors don't access the API. If this invariant is broken, use-after-free occurs.
findings.fix_suggestion: Document the destruction ordering requirement in a comment. Verify that MdSpiImpl/TdSpiImpl destructors are trivial (default-generated) and don't access API pointers.

findings.title: _start_workers() override is a no-op that just calls base
findings.description: Lines 62-64 override _start_workers() but only call the base implementation. This override serves no purpose and could be removed.
findings.fix_suggestion: Remove the _start_workers() override from CtpGateway unless custom worker startup is needed.

findings.title: instruments_.insert at lines 111, 154 could be optimized with reserve
findings.description: Each insert may cause vector reallocation. The total size is unknown but could be estimated from response sizes.
findings.fix_suggestion: After extracting ids, reserve space in instruments_: `instruments_.reserve(instruments_.size() + ids.size());` before inserting.

findings.title: instruments_.clear() called twice (lines 68 and 75)
findings.description: Line 68 clears instruments_ before the loop, and line 75 clears it again at the start of each retry iteration. The first clear is redundant.
findings.fix_suggestion: Remove line 68 since line 75 handles it at the start of the first iteration.

findings.title: init_ctp() could return bool instead of void
findings.description: The function returns void and uses exceptions/internal state for error reporting. Returning bool would make success/failure explicit and allow the caller to decide whether to proceed.
findings.fix_suggestion: Change signature to `bool init_ctp()` and return false on failure. Caller (start()) can then decide to throw or continue.

findings.title: resolve_instruments() could return bool instead of void
findings.description: Similar to init_ctp(), returning bool would make the outcome explicit and allow the caller to handle empty resolution.
findings.fix_suggestion: Change signature to `bool resolve_instruments()` and return false if max retries exhausted with no instruments.

findings.title: on_quote_received could be marked noexcept
findings.description: The function catches all exceptions internally. Marking it noexcept would document this contract and enable compiler optimizations.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add noexcept specifier: `void on_quote_received(const tyche::Payload& payload) noexcept`.

findings.title: OnRtnDepthMarketData could be marked noexcept
findings.description: CTP callbacks should generally not throw. Marking them noexcept documents this contract.
findings.fix_suggestion: Mark CTP callback overrides as noexcept where appropriate.

findings.title: do_login could be marked noexcept
findings.description: do_login performs C API calls and atomic increments that don't throw. Marking it noexcept documents the contract.
findings.fix_suggestion: Mark do_login() as noexcept.

findings.title: MdSpiImpl members md_api_ and on_quote_cb_ never change after construction but aren't const
findings.description: md_api_ and on_quote_cb_ are set in the constructor and never modified. Marking them const would prevent accidental mutation and enable compiler optimizations.
findings.fix_suggestion: Change to `CThostFtdcMdApi* const md_api_;` and `const QuoteCallback on_quote_cb_;` if move semantics are not needed.

findings.title: Range-based for with structured bindings is modern and readable
findings.description: Line 78 uses `auto& [exchange, products]` which is good C++17. The code correctly uses references to avoid copies.
findings.fix_suggestion: No change needed. Good modern C++ practice.

findings.title: std::any_cast<const std::string&> used for zero-copy access
findings.description: Lines 96, 200, 209 use reference any_casts to avoid string copies. This is correct when the any outlives the reference.
findings.fix_suggestion: No change needed.

findings.title: option_dropped_count_ and option_err_count_ use relaxed memory ordering
findings.description: Lines 303, 340, 350, 351 use memory_order_relaxed for counters. This is correct for simple statistics where exact ordering doesn't matter.
findings.fix_suggestion: No change needed. Good use of relaxed ordering for statistics.

findings.title: stop() follows correct shutdown order
findings.description: Cleanup CTP, notify thread, join thread, stop base. This ordering is correct and safe.
findings.fix_suggestion: No change needed.

findings.title: cleanup_ctp() uses exchange for idempotency
findings.description: Line 374 uses `cleanup_done_.exchange(true)` to ensure cleanup runs at most once. This is a good pattern.
findings.fix_suggestion: No change needed.

findings.title: validate_dll_path adds security checks
findings.description: The function checks for path traversal, empty names, path separators in filenames, and valid extensions. This is a good security improvement.
findings.fix_suggestion: No change needed. Good security practice.

findings.title: find_export_containing has improved bounds checking
findings.description: The PE export parsing now validates image bounds, export directory bounds, array bounds, and string lengths. This addresses the previous out-of-bounds read concern.
findings.fix_suggestion: No change needed. The bounds checking is now adequate.

findings.title: Signal handler comments document async-signal-safety concerns
findings.description: The comment at line 20-22 acknowledges that the handler uses non-async-signal-safe operations. This is honest documentation of a known limitation.
findings.fix_suggestion: Consider refactoring to use only async-signal-safe operations (e.g., write() to a pipe instead of std::cout, and set a flag for main thread to call stop()).

findings.title: g_gateway now uses atomic store/load
findings.description: Lines 17, 99, 105 use std::atomic for g_gateway. This is an improvement over the raw pointer but doesn't fully solve the lifetime race.
findings.fix_suggestion: Address via the RAII scope guard recommendation above.

findings.title: Structured bindings used for JSON iteration
findings.description: Line 51 uses `auto& [exchange, products]` for nlohmann::json iteration. This is clean modern C++.
findings.fix_suggestion: No change needed.

findings.title: GatewayConfig uses default member initializers
findings.description: All fields have sensible defaults. This is good modern C++ practice that avoids uninitialized member bugs.
findings.fix_suggestion: No change needed.

findings.title: md_front_mut_ and td_front_mut_ are mutable copies for CTP API
findings.description: Lines 81-82 create mutable string copies so that RegisterFront can receive non-const char*. This is a safe alternative to const_cast. Good improvement.
findings.fix_suggestion: No change needed. This is a correct and safe pattern.

findings.title: started_ atomic flag prevents double-start
findings.description: Line 37 uses `started_.exchange(true)` to prevent start() from being called twice. This is a good defensive pattern.
findings.fix_suggestion: No change needed.

findings.title: option_dispatch_loop uses condition_variable wait_for with predicate
findings.description: Line 325-326 uses wait_for with a predicate lambda. This correctly handles spurious wakeups. Good practice.
findings.fix_suggestion: No change needed.

findings.title: Queue pop uses move semantics
findings.description: Line 331 moves the front element before popping. This avoids copying the Payload. Good practice.
findings.fix_suggestion: No change needed.

findings.title: option_queue_cv_.notify_one() called outside lock
findings.description: Line 309 calls notify_one() after releasing the lock. This is the recommended pattern to avoid waking a thread that immediately blocks again.
findings.fix_suggestion: No change needed.

findings.title: option_instruments_ read uses shared_lock
findings.description: Line 287 uses std::shared_lock for concurrent reads. This is correct if there are potential writers, though currently there are none after start.
findings.fix_suggestion: No change needed.

findings.title: option_instruments_ write uses unique_lock
findings.description: Line 157 uses std::unique_lock when inserting into option_instruments_. This is correct for writer access.
findings.fix_suggestion: No change needed.

findings.title: TdApi init catch block now cleans up partial state
findings.description: Lines 238-244 clean up td_api_ and td_spi_ on TdApi init failure. This is an improvement over earlier versions.
findings.fix_suggestion: No change needed. The cleanup is now present.

findings.title: MdApi init catch block now cleans up partial state
findings.description: Lines 264-270 clean up md_api_ and md_spi_ on MdApi init failure. This is an improvement.
findings.fix_suggestion: No change needed.

findings.title: extract_instrument_ids now throws on malformed data instead of swallowing
findings.description: The function no longer catches bad_any_cast internally. It lets exceptions propagate to the caller. This is a design choice — fail-fast vs graceful degradation.
findings.fix_suggestion: Document that extract_instrument_ids throws on malformed responses and that callers must handle exceptions.

findings.title: Retry loop uses linear backoff
findings.description: The retry uses fixed 5-second intervals. Exponential backoff with jitter would be more network-friendly.
findings.fix_suggestion: Consider exponential backoff: `sleep_ms = min(max_sleep_ms, base_sleep_ms * (1 << attempt));` with random jitter.

findings.title: std::this_thread::sleep_for is modern and correct
findings.description: Line 181 uses std::this_thread::sleep_for with chrono literals. This is the correct modern C++ approach.
findings.fix_suggestion: No change needed.

findings.title: request_event timeout cast from int to float
findings.description: Line 90 casts cfg_.static_data_timeout_secs to float. For timeout values, this is safe and well-defined.
findings.fix_suggestion: No change needed.

findings.title: option_instruments_.count() could use contains() in C++20
findings.description: Line 288 uses count() > 0. In C++20, contains() is more readable.
findings.fix_suggestion: If compiling with C++20, use contains(). Otherwise, keep count().

findings.title: instrument_id string copy on every tick
findings.description: Line 281 copies the instrument_id from the payload. For high-frequency data, this allocates memory.
findings.fix_suggestion: Use string_view for the lookup if C++20 heterogeneous lookup is available in unordered_set. Otherwise, document the copy as acceptable.

findings.title: Payload copy into queue instead of move
findings.description: Line 297 copies payload into the queue. As noted earlier, accepting by value and moving would be more efficient.
findings.fix_suggestion: Change callback signature to accept by value if the TycheModule API allows it.

findings.title: Queue overflow drops newest ticks
findings.description: When full, new ticks are dropped. For market data, dropping oldest is usually preferred.
findings.fix_suggestion: Use a circular buffer with overwrite semantics, or pop the oldest before pushing the newest when full.

findings.title: stop() is idempotent via cleanup_ctp() guard
findings.description: cleanup_ctp() uses cleanup_done_.exchange(true) to prevent double-cleanup. stop() can be called multiple times safely.
findings.fix_suggestion: No change needed.

findings.title: cleanup_ctp() logs to cout which may be closed
findings.description: If stdout is closed during late shutdown, std::cout << may crash. This is a corner case.
findings.fix_suggestion: Use a logging system that handles late-stage shutdown, or suppress output if shutdown is in progress.

findings.title: SPI reset after API release assumes destructor is trivial
findings.description: md_spi_.reset() at line 389 happens after md_api_->Release() at line 381. If MdSpiImpl destructor accesses md_api_, this is use-after-free.
findings.fix_suggestion: Verify MdSpiImpl has a default destructor that doesn't access md_api_. Document this invariant.

findings.title: TdApi login timeout logged but not exposed to monitoring
findings.description: The timeout is logged to stderr but operators may not monitor stderr. No metric or health check reflects the TdApi connection state.
findings.fix_suggestion: Expose TdApi connection status via a health endpoint or metric. Consider making it fatal if td_front is explicitly configured.

findings.title: start() has strict ordering documented in comments
findings.description: The comment at lines 42-58 documents the startup sequence. This is good but not enforced by the type system.
findings.fix_suggestion: Consider using a state machine enum to enforce valid transitions.

findings.title: resolve_instruments has no separation between I/O and business logic
findings.description: The function directly calls request_event() and processes responses. This makes unit testing impossible without a running engine.
findings.fix_suggestion: Inject a query interface or use a mockable dependency for request_event.

findings.title: on_quote_received mixes routing logic with queue management
findings.description: The function decides routing (futures vs options) and manages queue locking/pushing. These are two responsibilities.
findings.fix_suggestion: Extract `enqueue_option_tick()` and `dispatch_futures_tick()` helpers.

findings.title: option_dispatch_loop hardcodes event name and timeout
findings.description: Lines 337 uses "compute_greeks" and 10.0f. These should be named constants or configurable.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add static constexpr members or config fields.

findings.title: extract_instrument_ids is static but could be a free function
findings.description: The function doesn't access instance state. It could be a free function in an anonymous namespace in ctp_gateway.cpp.
findings.fix_suggestion: Move to anonymous namespace to reduce class bloat.

findings.title: File is 393 lines — acceptable but could be split
findings.description: ctp_gateway.cpp is approaching the large file threshold. It contains instrument resolution, CTP lifecycle, quote routing, and option dispatch.
findings.fix_suggestion: Consider splitting into ctp_gateway_instruments.cpp, ctp_gateway_lifecycle.cpp, ctp_gateway_dispatch.cpp.

findings.title: md_spi.cpp is well-organized at 162 lines
findings.description: The file has clear separation of concerns: login, subscribe, callbacks, and conversion.
findings.fix_suggestion: No change needed.

findings.title: td_spi.cpp is concise and well-organized
findings.description: The file is 120 lines with clear authentication and login flow.
findings.fix_suggestion: No change needed.

findings.title: config.cpp is focused and readable
findings.description: The file handles JSON parsing with clear error messages. Good structure.
findings.fix_suggestion: No change needed.

findings.title: ctp_loader.cpp has good platform separation
findings.description: Windows and Linux code are cleanly separated with #ifdef. The comments explain TTS compatibility well.
findings.fix_suggestion: No change needed.

findings.title: main.cpp has clear lifecycle management
findings.description: Argument parsing, signal setup, config loading, and gateway lifecycle are well-separated.
findings.fix_suggestion: No change needed.
